item.name: payment request percentage
item.params: WITH last_50000 AS ( SELECT id, requestdate FROM "PaymentGatewayRequest" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 50000 ), time_refs AS ( SELECT date_trunc('minute', NOW() - INTERVAL '1 minute') AS prev_minute, date_trunc('minute', NOW() - INTERVAL '1 week 1 minute') AS prev_week_same_minute ), current_minute_count AS ( SELECT COUNT(*) AS request_count FROM last_50000 l, time_refs t WHERE date_trunc('minute', l.requestdate) between t.prev_minute - interval '10 minutes' and t.prev_minute ), historical_minute_sum AS ( SELECT SUM(requests)/6 AS historical_requests FROM RequestCounts r, time_refs t WHERE r.requestdate_trunc = date_trunc('hour', t.prev_week_same_minute) ) SELECT c.request_count/h.historical_requests::float as percent FROM current_minute_count c, historical_minute_sum h;

item.description: Number of disk blocks read per second

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: registered conflicts

item.description: Number of deadlocks detected.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: temp_bytes written
item.description: Total amount of data written to temporary files by queries. All temporary files are counted, regardless of why the temporary file was created, and regardless of the log_temp_files setting.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: temp_files created
item.description: Number of temporary files created by queries. All temporary files are counted, regardless of why the temporary file was created (e.g., sorting or hashing), and regardless of the log_temp_files setting.

item.description: Check cache hit ratio in %.

item.name: PostgreSQL: number of active connections
item.description: Total count of active clients who connected to the PostgreSQL service and performing work.

item.description: Total count of idle clients connected to the PostgreSQL service.

item.name: PostgreSQL: pg_stat_activity (internal item)
item.description: Internal item to fetch all pg_stat_activity values in a single call

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: blocks hit per second
item.description: Number of times disk blocks were found already in the buffer cache, so that a read was not necessary (this only includes hits in the PostgreSQL buffer cache, not the operating system's file system cache)

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: blocks read per second
item.description: Number of disk blocks read per second

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: registered conflicts
item.description: Number of queries canceled due to conflicts with recovery in this database. (Conflicts occur only on standby servers; see pg_stat_database_conflicts for details.).

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: registered deadlocks
item.description: Number of deadlocks detected.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: temp_bytes written
item.description: Total amount of data written to temporary files by queries. All temporary files are counted, regardless of why the temporary file was created, and regardless of the log_temp_files setting.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: temp_files created
item.description: Number of temporary files created by queries. All temporary files are counted, regardless of why the temporary file was created (e.g., sorting or hashing), and regardless of the log_temp_files setting.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: tuples deleted per second
item.description: Number of rows deleted by queries.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: tuples fetched per second
item.description: Number of rows fetched by queries.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: tuples inserted per second
item.description: Number of rows inserted by queries.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: tuples returned per second
item.description: Number of rows returned by queries.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: tuples updated per second
item.description: Number of rows updated by queries.

item.name: PostgresSQL dbstat: commits per second
item.description: Number of transactions that have been committed.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat: rollbacks per second
item.description: Number of transactions that have been rolled back.

item.name: PostgreSQL dbstat (internal item)
item.description: Internal item to fetch all pg_stat_database sum values in a single call

template.template: Template Module Cisco CISCO-PROCESS-MIB IOS versions 12.0_3_T-12.2_3.5 SNMPv2
template.name: Template Module Cisco CISCO-PROCESS-MIB IOS versions 12.0_3_T-12.2_3.5 SNMPv2
template.description: Template Module Cisco CISCO-PROCESS-MIB IOS versions 12.0_3_T-12.2_3.5 version: 0.13 MIBs used: CISCO-PROCESS-MIB

discovery_rule.name: CPU Discovery
discovery_rule.description: If your IOS device has several CPUs, you must use CISCO-PROCESS-MIB and its object cpmCPUTotal5minRev from the table called cpmCPUTotalTable , indexed with cpmCPUTotalIndex . This table allows CISCO-PROCESS-MIB to keep CPU statistics for different physical entities in the router, like different CPU chips, group of CPUs, or CPUs in different modules/cards. In case of a single CPU, cpmCPUTotalTable has only one entry.

item_prototype.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: CPU utilization
item_prototype.description: MIB: CISCO-PROCESS-MIB The overall CPU busy percentage in the last 5 minute period. This object deprecates the avgBusy5 object from the OLD-CISCO-SYSTEM-MIB. This object is deprecated by cpmCPUTotal5minRev which has the changed range of value (0..100) Reference: http://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/ip/simple-network-management-protocol-snmp/15215-collect-cpu-util-snmp.html

trigger_prototype.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: High CPU utilization
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}.

item.host: Template Module Cisco CISCO-PROCESS-MIB IOS versions 12.0_3_T-12.2_3.5 SNMPv2

template.name: Template Module Cisco CISCO-PROCESS-MIB SNMPv2
template.description: Template Cisco CISCO-PROCESS-MIB version: 0.13 MIBs used: CISCO-PROCESS-MIB

discovery_rule.name: CPU Discovery
discovery_rule.description: If your IOS device has several CPUs, you must use CISCO-PROCESS-MIB and its object cpmCPUTotal5minRev from the table called cpmCPUTotalTable , indexed with cpmCPUTotalIndex . This table allows CISCO-PROCESS-MIB to keep CPU statistics for different physical entities in the router, like different CPU chips, group of CPUs, or CPUs in different modules/cards. In case of a single CPU, cpmCPUTotalTable has only one entry.

item_prototype.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: CPU utilization
item_prototype.description: MIB: CISCO-PROCESS-MIB The cpmCPUTotal5minRev MIB object provides a more accurate view of the performance of the router over time than the MIB objects cpmCPUTotal1minRev and cpmCPUTotal5secRev . These MIB objects are not accurate because they look at CPU at one minute and five second intervals, respectively. These MIBs enable you to monitor the trends and plan the capacity of your network. The recommended baseline rising threshold for cpmCPUTotal5minRev is 90 percent. Depending on the platform, some routers that run at 90 percent, for example, 2500s, can exhibit performance degradation versus a high-end router, for example, the 7500 series, which can operate fine. Reference: http://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/ip/simple-network-management-protocol-snmp/15215-collect-cpu-util-snmp.html

trigger_prototype.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: High CPU utilization
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}.

template.description: Template Module Generic version: 0.13 MIBs used: SNMPv2-MIB

item.name: SNMP traps (fallback)
item.description: Item is used to collect all SNMP traps unmatched by other snmptrap items

item.name: Device contact details
item.description: MIB: SNMPv2-MIB The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person. If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

item.name: Device description
item.description: MIB: SNMPv2-MIB A textual description of the entity. This value should include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and networking software.

item.name: Device location
item.description: MIB: SNMPv2-MIB The physical location of this node (e.g., `telephone closet, 3rd floor'). If the location is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

item.name: Device name
item.description: MIB: SNMPv2-MIB An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

item.name: System object ID
item.description: MIB: SNMPv2-MIB The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed. For example, if vendor`Flintstones, Inc.' was assigned the subtree1.3.6.1.4.1.4242, it could assign the identifier 1.3.6.1.4.1.4242.1.1 to its `Fred Router'.

item.name: Device uptime
item.description: MIB: SNMPv2-MIB The time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

trigger.name: {HOST.NAME} has been restarted
trigger.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. The device uptime is less than 10 minutes

trigger.name: No SNMP data collection
trigger.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

item_prototype.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: Total memory in units
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The size of the storage represented by this entry, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits. This object is writable to allow remote configuration of the size of the storage area in those cases where such an operation makes sense and is possible on the underlying system. For example, the amount of main memory allocated to a buffer pool might be modified or the amount of disk space allocated to virtual memory might be modified.

item_prototype.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: Used memory in units
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

item_prototype.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: Memory units
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The size, in bytes, of the data objects allocated from this pool. If this entry is monitoring sectors, blocks, buffers, or packets, for example, this number will commonly be greater than one. Otherwise this number will typically be one.

item_prototype.description: Total space in Bytes

item_prototype.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Total space in units
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The size of the storage represented by this entry, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits. This object is writable to allow remote configuration of the size of the storage area in those cases where such an operation makes sense and is possible on the underlying system. For example, the amount of main storage allocated to a buffer pool might be modified or the amount of disk space allocated to virtual storage might be modified.

item_prototype.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Used storage in units
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

item_prototype.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Storage units
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The size, in bytes, of the data objects allocated from this pool. If this entry is monitoring sectors, blocks, buffers, or packets, for example, this number will commonly be greater than one. Otherwise this number will typically be one.

item_prototype.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Used space
item_prototype.description: Used storage in Bytes

item.name: CPU utilization

item.description: MIB: SNMPv2-MIB

item.name: Available memory
item.description: MIB: TIMETRA-SYSTEM-MIB The value of sgiKbMemoryAvailable indicates the amount of freememory, in kilobytes, in the overall system that is not allocated to memory pools, but is available in case a memory pool needs to grow.

item.name: Memory utilization
item.params: (last(vm.memory.used[sgiKbMemoryUsed.0])/(last(vm.memory.free[sgiKbMemoryAvailable.0])+last(vm.memory.used[sgiKbMemoryUsed.0])))*100
item.description: Memory utilization in %

item.name: Used memory
item.description: MIB: TIMETRA-SYSTEM-MIB The value of sgiKbMemoryUsed indicates the total pre-allocated pool memory, in kilobytes, currently in use on the system.

item.name: Overall system health status
item.description: MIB: SW-MIB The current operational status of the switch.The states are as follow: online(1) means the switch is accessible by an external Fibre Channel port offline(2) means the switch is not accessible testing(3) means the switch is in a built-in test mode and is not accessible by an external Fibre Channel port faulty(4) means the switch is not operational.

template.name: Template Net Brocade_Foundry Nonstackable SNMPv2
template.description:
Template Net Brocade_Foundry Nonstackable version: 0.13 Overview: For devices(old Foundry devices, MLXe and so on) that doesn't support Stackable SNMP Tables: snChasFan2Table, snChasPwrSupply2Table,snAgentTemp2Table - FOUNDRY-SN-AGENT-MIB::snChasFanTable, snChasPwrSupplyTable,snAgentTempTable are used instead. For example: The objects in table snChasPwrSupply2Table is not supported on the NetIron and the FastIron SX devices. snChasFan2Table is not supported on on the NetIron devices. snAgentTemp2Table is not supported on old versions of MLXe
MIBs used: FOUNDRY-SN-AGENT-MIB

Template Brocade_Foundry Stackable version: 0.13 Overview: For devices(most of the IronWare Brocade devices) that support Stackable SNMP Tables in FOUNDRY-SN-AGENT-MIB: snChasFan2Table, snChasPwrSupply2Table,snAgentTemp2Table - so objects from all Stack members are provided.
MIBs used: FOUNDRY-SN-AGENT-MIB FOUNDRY-SN-STACKING-MIB Known Issues: description : Correct fan(returns fan status as 'other(1)' and temperature (returns 0) for the non-master Switches are not available in SNMP version : Version 08.0.40b and above device : ICX 7750 in stack

item_prototype.description: MIB: EQUIPMENT-MIB Indicates the current fan status. speed-0 : If the fan function is normal and the fan does not spin due to the temperature not reaching the threshold, the status of the fan is speed 0. speed-low : Fan spin using the lowest speed. speed-middle: Fan spin using the middle speed. speed-high : Fan spin using the highest speed.

discovery_rule.name: PSU Discovery
discovery_rule.description: A textual description of the power supply, that can be assigned by the administrator.

item_prototype.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Power supply status
item_prototype.description: MIB: ICS-CHASSIS-MIB Actual status of the power supply: (1) unknown: status not known. (2) disabled: power supply is disabled. (3) failed - power supply is unable to supply power due to failure. (4) warning - power supply is supplying power, but an output or sensor is bad or warning. (5) standby - power supply believed usable,but not supplying power. (6) engaged - power supply is supplying power. (7) redundant - power supply is supplying power, but not needed. (8) notPresent - power supply is supplying power is not present.

trigger_prototype.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Power supply is in critical state
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. Please check the power supply unit for errors

item.name: Hardware model name
item.description: MIB: JUNIPER-MIB The name, model, or detailed description of the box,indicating which product the box is about, for example 'M40'.

item.description: MIB: JUNIPER-MIB The serial number of this subject, blank if unknown or unavailable.

trigger.name: Device has been replaced (new serial number received)
trigger.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. Device serial number has changed. Ack to close

item.name: Overall system health status
item.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-ALARM-MIB The red alarm indication on the craft interface panel. The red alarm is on when there is some system failure or power supply failure or the system is experiencing a hardware malfunction or some threshold is being exceeded.
This red alarm state could be turned off by the ACO/LT (Alarm Cut Off / Lamp Test) button on the front panel module.

trigger.name: System status is in critical state
trigger.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. Please check the device for errors

item.description: MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB Software version

item.name: Memory utilization
item.params: (last(vm.memory.used[hrStorageUsed.Memory])/last(vm.memory.total[hrStorageSize.Memory]))*100
item.description: Memory utilization in %

trigger.name: High memory utilization
trigger.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}.

item.name: Total memory
item.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The size of the storage represented by this entry, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits. This object is writable to allow remote configuration of the size of the storage area in those cases where such an operation makes sense and is possible on the underlying system. For example, the amount of main memory allocated to a buffer pool might be modified or the amount of disk space allocated to virtual memory might be modified.

item.name: Used memory
item.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

discovery_rule.name: CPU Discovery
discovery_rule.description: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB::hrProcessorTable discovery

item_prototype.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: CPU utilization
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The average, over the last minute, of the percentage of time that this processor was not idle.Implementations may approximate this one minute smoothing period if necessary.

trigger_prototype.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: High CPU utilization
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}.

discovery_rule.name: Temperature Discovery CPU
discovery_rule.description: MIKROTIK-MIB::mtxrHlProcessorTemperature Since temperature of CPU is not available on all Mikrotik hardware, this is done to avoid unsupported items.

item_prototype.name: CPU: Temperature
item_prototype.description: MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB (mtxrHlProcessorTemperature) Processor temperature in Celsius (degrees C). Might be missing in entry models (RB750, RB450G..)

trigger_prototype.name: CPU: Temperature is above critical threshold: >{$TEMP_CRIT:"CPU"}
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as temperature sensor status if available

trigger_prototype.name: CPU: Temperature is above warning threshold: >{$TEMP_WARN:"CPU"}
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as temperature sensor status if available

dependency.name: CPU: Temperature is above critical threshold: >{$TEMP_CRIT:"CPU"}

trigger_prototype.name: CPU: Temperature is too low: <{$TEMP_CRIT_LOW:"CPU"}
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}.

discovery_rule.name: Storage Discovery
discovery_rule.description: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB::hrStorage discovery with storage filter

item_prototype.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Storage utilization
item_prototype.description: Storage utilization in % for Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}

trigger_prototype.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Disk space is critically low
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}.

trigger_prototype.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Disk space is low
trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}.

dependency.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Disk space is critically low

item_prototype.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Total space
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The size of the storage represented by this entry, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits. This object is writable to allow remote configuration of the size of the storage area in those cases where such an operation makes sense and is possible on the underlying system. For example, the amount of main memory allocated to a buffer pool might be modified or the amount of disk space allocated to virtual memory might be modified.

item_prototype.name: Disk-{#SNMPINDEX}: Used space
item_prototype.description: MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

template.name: Template Net Netgear Fastpath SNMPv2

Template Net Netgear Fastpath version: 0.13 Overview: https://kb.netgear.com/24352/MIBs-for-Smart-switches

item.description: Memory utilization in %

item_prototype.description: MIB: FASTPATH-BOXSERVICES-PRIVATE-MIB The temperature value reported by sensor

trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}.

trigger_prototype.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as temperature sensor status if available

template.name: Template Net TP-LINK SNMPv2
template.description:
Template Net TP-LINK JetStream version: 0.13 Overview: Link to MIBs: http://www.tp-linkru.com/download/T2600G-28TS.html#MIBs_Files Sample device overview page: http://www.tp-linkru.com/products/details/cat-39_T2600G-28TS.html#overview emulation page(web): http://www.tp-linkru.com/resources/simulator/T2600G-28TS(UN)_1.0/Index.htm
MIBs used: TPLINK-SYSMONITOR-MIB TPLINK-SYSINFO-MIB Known Issues: description : default sysLocation, sysName and sysContact is not filled with proper data. Real hostname and location can be found only in private branch(TPLINK-SYSINFO-MIB). Please check whether this problem exists in the latest firware: http://www.tp-linkru.com/download/T2600G-28TS.html#Firmware version : 2.0.0 Build 20170628 Rel.55184(Beta) device : T2600G-28TS 2.0description : The Serial number of the product(tpSysInfoSerialNum) is missing in HW versions prior to V2_170323 version : prior to version V2_170323 device : T2600G-28TS 2.0

item.name: Firmware version
item.description: MIB: TPLINK-SYSINFO-MIB The software version of the product.

item.description: MIB: TPLINK-SYSINFO-MIB The hardware version of the product.

item.description: MIB: TPLINK-SYSINFO-MIB The Serial number of the product.

trigger.description: Last value: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}. Device serial number has changed. Ack to close

template.name: Template Net Ubiquiti AirOS SNMPv1
template.description: Template Net Ubiquiti AirOS version: 0.13 MIBs used: FROGFOOT-RESOURCES-MIB IEEE802dot11-MIB Known Issues: description : UBNT unifi reports speed: like IF-MIB::ifSpeed.1 = Gauge32: 4294967295 for all interfaces version : Firmware: BZ.ar7240.v3.7.51.6230.170322.1513 device : UBNT UAP-LRdescription : UBNT AirMax(NanoStation, NanoBridge etc) reports ifSpeed: as 0 for VLAN and wireless(ath0) interfaces version : Firmware: XW.ar934x.v5.6-beta4.22359.140521.1836 device : NanoStation M5description : UBNT AirMax(NanoStation, NanoBridge etc) reports always return ifType: as ethernet(6) even for wifi,vlans and other types version : Firmware: XW.ar934x.v5.6-beta4.22359.140521.1836 device : NanoStation M5description : ifXTable is not provided in IF-MIB. So Interfaces Simple Template is used instead version : all above device : NanoStation, UAP-LR

template.name: Template OS Windows SNMPv2
template.description: Template OS Windows version: 0.13 Known Issues: description : Doesn't support In/Out 64 bit counters even thoug1h IfxTable is present: Currently, Windows gets it’s interface status from MIB-2. Since these 64bit SNMP counters (ifHCInOctets, ifHCOutOctets, etc.) are defined as an extension to IF-MIB, Microsoft has not implemented it. https://social.technet.microsoft.com/Forums/windowsserver/en-US/07b62ff0-94f6-40ca-a99d-d129c1b33d70/windows-2008-r2-snmp-64bit-counters-support?forum=winservergen version : Win2008, Win2012R2 device : description : Doesn't support ifXTable at all version : WindowsXP device : description : EtherLike MIB is not supported version : * device : *

dependency.name: BB +1.8V SM Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_1.8v_sm.last(0)}<1.597 or {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_1.8v_sm.last(0)}>2.019

dependency.name: BB +3.3V Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_3.3v.last(0)}<2.876 or {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_3.3v.last(0)}>3.729

dependency.name: BB +3.3V STBY Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_3.3v_stby.last(0)}<2.876 or {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_3.3v_stby.last(0)}>3.729

dependency.name: BB +5.0V Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_5.0v.last(0)}<4.362 or {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_5.0v.last(0)}>5.663

dependency.name: BB Ambient Temp Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_ambient_temp.last(0)}<5 or {Template Server Intel SR1530 IPMI:bb_ambient_temp.last(0)}>66

dependency.name: Baseboard Temp Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:baseboard_temp.last(0)}<5 or {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:baseboard_temp.last(0)}>90

dependency.name: BB +1.05V PCH Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_1.05v_pch.last(0)}<0.953 or {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_1.05v_pch.last(0)}>1.149

dependency.name: BB +1.1V P1 Vccp Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_1.1v_p1_vccp.last(0)}<0.683 or {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_1.1v_p1_vccp.last(0)}>1.543

dependency.name: BB +1.5V P1 DDR3 Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_1.5v_p1_ddr3.last(0)}<1.362 or {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_1.5v_p1_ddr3.last(0)}>1.635

dependency.name: BB +3.3V Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_3.3v.last(0)}<2.982 or {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_3.3v.last(0)}>3.625

dependency.name: BB +3.3V STBY Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_3.3v_stby.last(0)}<2.982 or {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_3.3v_stby.last(0)}>3.625

dependency.name: BB +5.0V Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_5.0v.last(0)}<4.471 or {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:bb_5.0v.last(0)}>5.538

dependency.name: Front Panel Temp Critical [{ITEM.VALUE}]
dependency.expression: {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:front_panel_temp.last(0)}<0 or {Template Server Intel SR1630 IPMI:front_panel_temp.last(0)}>48

discovery_rule.name: Discover VMware clusters
discovery_rule.description: Discovery of clusters

discovery_rule.name: Discover VMware hypervisors
discovery_rule.description: Discovery of hypervisors.

discovery_rule.name: Discover VMware VMs
discovery_rule.description: Discovery of guest virtual machines.

item.name: Cluster name
item.description: Cluster name of the guest VM.

item.name: Number of virtual CPUs
item.description: Number of virtual CPUs assigned to the guest.

item.name: CPU ready
item.description: Time that the virtual machine was ready, but could not get scheduled to run on the physical CPU during last measurement interval (VMware vCenter/ESXi Server performance counter sampling interval - 20 seconds)

item.name: CPU usage
item.description: Current upper-bound on CPU usage. The upper-bound is based on the host the virtual machine is current running on, as well as limits configured on the virtual machine itself or any parent resource pool. Valid while the virtual machine is running.

item.name: Datacenter name
item.description: Datacenter name of the guest VM.

item.name: Hypervisor name
item.description: Hypervisor name of the guest VM.

item.name: Ballooned memory
item.description: The amount of guest physical memory that is currently reclaimed through the balloon driver.

item.name: Compressed memory
item.description: The amount of memory currently in the compression cache for this VM.

item.name: Private memory
item.description: Amount of memory backed by host memory and not being shared.

item.name: Shared memory
item.description: The amount of guest physical memory shared through transparent page sharing.

item.name: Swapped memory
item.description: The amount of guest physical memory swapped out to the VM's swap device by ESX.

item.name: Guest memory usage
item.description: The amount of guest physical memory that is being used by the VM.

item.name: Host memory usage
item.description: The amount of host physical memory allocated to the VM, accounting for saving from memory sharing with other VMs.

item.name: Memory size
item.description: Total size of configured memory.

item.name: Power state
item.description: The current power state of the virtual machine.

item.name: Committed storage space
item.description: Total storage space, in bytes, committed to this virtual machine across all datastores.

item.name: Uncommitted storage space
item.description: Additional storage space, in bytes, potentially used by this virtual machine on all datastores.

item.name: Unshared storage space
item.description: Total storage space, in bytes, occupied by the virtual machine across all datastores, that is not shared with any other virtual machine.

item.name: Uptime
item.description: System uptime.

discovery_rule.name: Network device discovery
discovery_rule.description: Discovery of all network devices.

item_prototype.name: Number of bytes received on interface {#IFDESC}
Number of packets received on interface {#IFDESC}
Number of bytes transmitted on interface {#IFDESC}
Number of packets transmitted on interface {#IFDESC}

discovery_rule.name: Disk device discovery
discovery_rule.description: Discovery of all disk devices.

item_prototype.name: Average number of bytes read from the disk {#DISKDESC}
Average number of reads from the disk {#DISKDESC}
Average number of bytes written to the disk {#DISKDESC}
Average number of writes to the disk {#DISKDESC}

discovery_rule.name: Mounted filesystem discovery
discovery_rule.description: Discovery of all guest file systems.

item.name: Cluster name
item.description: Cluster name of the guest VM.

item.name: CPU usage
item.description: Aggregated CPU usage across all cores on the host in Hz. This is only available if the host is connected.

item.name: Datacenter name
item.description: Datacenter name of the hypervisor.

item.name: Full name
item.description: The complete product name, including the version information.

item.name: CPU frequency
item.description: The speed of the CPU cores. This is an average value if there are multiple speeds. The product of CPU frequency and number of cores is approximately equal to the sum of the MHz for all the individual cores on the host.

item.name: CPU model
item.description: The CPU model.

item.name: CPU cores
item.description: Number of physical CPU cores on the host. Physical CPU cores are the processors contained by a CPU package.

item.name: CPU threads
item.description: Number of physical CPU threads on the host.

item.name: Total memory
item.description: The physical memory size.

item.name: Model
item.description: The system model identification.

item.name: Bios UUID
item.description: The hardware BIOS identification.

item.name: Vendor
item.description: The hardware vendor identification.

item.name: Ballooned memory
item.description: The amount of guest physical memory that is currently reclaimed through the balloon driver. Sum of all guest VMs.

item.name: Used memory
item.description: Physical memory usage on the host.

item.name: Health state rollup
item.description: The host health state rollup sensor value: gray - unknown, ok, red - it has a problem, yellow - it might have a problem.

item.name: Overall status
item.description: The overall alarm status of the host: gray - unknown, ok, red - it has a problem, yellow - it might have a problem.

item.name: Uptime
item.description: System uptime.

item.name: Version
item.description: Dot-separated version string.

item.name: Number of guest VMs
item.description: Number of guest virtual machines.

item_prototype.name: Average read latency of the datastore $3
Average write latency of the datastore $3

item.name: 2 hrs
item.params: select count(*) from "Account" where accounttype = 0 and accountpackage_id <> 3 and installeddate notnull and status = 1 and clientversion >= '4.7' and lastclientinsertdate < now() - interval '2 hours' and id in (select account_id from "EnterpriseAccount" where status = 1 and enterprisecustomer_id != 12)

item.name: 4 hrs
item.params: select count(*) from "Account" where accounttype = 0 and accountpackage_id <> 3 and installeddate notnull and status = 1 and clientversion >= '4.7' and lastclientinsertdate < now() - interval '4 hours' and id in (select account_id from "EnterpriseAccount" where status = 1 and enterprisecustomer_id != 12)

item.params: select count(*) from "Account" where accounttype = 0 and accountpackage_id <> 3 and installeddate notnull and status = 1 and clientversion >= '4.7' and lastclientinsertdate < now() - interval '24 hours' /*and id in (select account_id from "EnterpriseAccount" where status = 1 and enterprisecustomer_id != 12)*/

item.params: select count(*) from "Account" where accounttype = 0 and accountpackage_id <> 3 and installeddate notnull and status = 1 and clientversion >= '4.7' and lastclientinsertdate < now() - interval '24 hours' and id in (select account_id from "EnterpriseAccount" where status = 1 and enterprisecustomer_id != 12)

item.name: total active accounts
item.params: select count(*) from "Account" where accounttype = 0 and accountpackage_id <> 3 and installeddate notnull and status = 1 and clientversion >= '4.7' and id in (select account_id from "EnterpriseAccount" where status = 1 and enterprisecustomer_id != 12)

item.name: facebook_reviews
item.params: select case when max(date_pulled) < now() - interval '3 days' then to_char(max(date_pulled)::timestamp at time zone 'utc' at time zone 'CST6CDT', 'Day FMMM/FMDD FMHH:MI AM') else 'good' end from reviews where sourceid = 5

trigger.name: Haven't Pulled Facebook Reviews Since {ITEM.LASTVALUE}
trigger.description: Last pulled a facebook review on {ITEM.VALUE}

item.name: google_reviews
item.params: select case when max(date_pulled) < now() - interval '3 days' then to_char(max(date_pulled)::timestamp at time zone 'utc' at time zone 'CST6CDT', 'Day FMMM/FMDD FMHH:MI AM') else 'good' end from reviews where sourceid = 4

item.name: test_reviews
item.params: select 'bad';

item.name: yellowpages_reviews
item.params: select case when max(date_pulled) < now() - interval '1 month' then to_char(max(date_pulled)::timestamp at time zone 'utc' at time zone 'CST6CDT', 'Day FMMM/FMDD FMHH:MI AM') else 'good' end from reviews where sourceid = 2

trigger.name: Haven't Pulled Yellow Pages Reviews Since {ITEM.LASTVALUE}

item.name: yelp_reviews
item.params: select case when max(date_pulled) < now() - interval '3 days' then to_char(max(date_pulled)::timestamp at time zone 'utc' at time zone 'CST6CDT', 'Day FMMM/FMDD FMHH:MI AM') else 'good' end from reviews where sourceid = 3

trigger.expression: {Template App Apache Tomcat JMX:jmx["Catalina:type=ThreadPool,name=http-8080",currentThreadsBusy].last(0)} > ({Template App Apache Tomcat JMX:jmx["Catalina:type=ThreadPool,name=http-8080",maxThreads].last(0)} * 0.7)
trigger.name: 70% http-8080 worker threads busy on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Apache Tomcat JMX:jmx["Catalina:type=ThreadPool,name=http-8443",currentThreadsBusy].last(0)} > ({Template App Apache Tomcat JMX:jmx["Catalina:type=ThreadPool,name=http-8443",maxThreads].last(0)} * 0.7)
trigger.name: 70% http-8443 worker threads busy on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Apache Tomcat JMX:jmx["Catalina:type=ThreadPool,name=jk-8009",currentThreadsBusy].last(0)} > ({Template App Apache Tomcat JMX:jmx["Catalina:type=ThreadPool,name=jk-8009",maxThreads].last(0)} *0.7)
trigger.name: 70% jk-8009 worker threads busy on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=Memory",HeapMemoryUsage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=Memory",HeapMemoryUsage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mem Heap Memory used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=Memory",NonHeapMemoryUsage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=Memory",NonHeapMemoryUsage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mem Non-Heap Memory used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=CMS Old Gen",Usage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=CMS Old Gen",Usage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mp CMS Old Gen used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=CMS Perm Gen",Usage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=CMS Perm Gen",Usage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mp CMS Perm Gen used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=Code Cache",Usage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=Code Cache",Usage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mp Code Cache used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=Perm Gen",Usage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=Perm Gen",Usage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mp Perm Gen used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=PS Old Gen",Usage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=PS Old Gen",Usage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mp PS Old Gen used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=PS Perm Gen",Usage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=PS Perm Gen",Usage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mp PS Perm Gen used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=Tenured Gen",Usage.used].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=Tenured Gen",Usage.max].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% mp Tenured Gen used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=OperatingSystem",OpenFileDescriptorCount].last(0)}>({Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=OperatingSystem",MaxFileDescriptorCount].last(0)}*0.7)
trigger.name: 70% os Opened File Descriptor Count used on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {vsmart sites:web.test.rspcode[main sites,Beta VSmart Login].last()}<>200 and {vsmart sites:web.test.rspcode[main sites,Beta VSmart Login].last(1)}<>200
trigger.name: Beta VSmart Login Gave Response {ITEM.VALUE}

dependency.name: Zabbix agent on {HOST.NAME} is unreachable for 5 minutes

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=GarbageCollector,name=ParNew",CollectionCount].last(0)}<{Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=GarbageCollector,name=ConcurrentMarkSweep",CollectionCount].last(0)}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=GarbageCollector,name=Copy",CollectionCount].last(0)}<{Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=GarbageCollector,name=MarkSweepCompact",CollectionCount].last(0)}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=GarbageCollector,name=PS Scavenge",CollectionCount].last(0)}<{Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=GarbageCollector,name=PS MarkSweep",CollectionCount].last(0)}
trigger.name: gc PS Mark Sweep in fire fighting mode on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=Tenured Gen",Usage.committed].last(0)}={Template App Generic Java JMX:jmx["java.lang:type=MemoryPool,name=Tenured Gen",Usage.max].last(0)}
trigger.name: mp Tenured Gen fully committed on {HOST.NAME}

trigger.expression: {VSmart Pulling Accounts:db.odbc.select[18hrs,vsmart].last()} / {VSmart Pulling Accounts:db.odbc.select[total_active,vsmart].last()} > .12 and {VSmart Pulling Accounts:db.odbc.select[18hrs,vsmart].dayofweek()}>1 and {VSmart Pulling Accounts:db.odbc.select[18hrs,vsmart].dayofweek()}<=5
trigger.name: VSmart Prod Too Many Accounts Not Pulling
trigger.description: {?{VSmart Pulling Accounts:db.odbc.select[18hrs,vsmart].last()}/{VSmart Pulling Accounts:db.odbc.select[total_active,vsmart].last()}*100}

trigger.expression: {Template DB PostgreSQL - BETA:wal.archived.last()} + {Template DB PostgreSQL - BETA:wal.waiting.last()} > 20
trigger.name: Wal Files on {HOSTNAME} > 20
trigger.description: There are {ITEM.LASTVALUE1} wal files that have been archived and waiting to be pushed to dbbackup. There are {ITEM.LASTVALUE2} wal files that are waiting to be archived by postgresql.

trigger.name: Wal Files on {HOSTNAME} > {$PG_WAL_THRESHOLD_HIGH}
trigger.description: There are {ITEM.LASTVALUE1} wal files that have been archived and waiting to be pushed to dbbackup. There are {ITEM.LASTVALUE2} wal files that are waiting to be archived by postgresql.

trigger.name: Wal Files on {HOSTNAME} >{$PG_WAL_THRESHOLD}
trigger.description: There are {ITEM.LASTVALUE1} wal files that have been archived and waiting to be pushed to dbbackup. There are {ITEM.LASTVALUE2} wal files that are waiting to be archived by postgresql.
